{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=phase&id=2579&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=phase&id=2579&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=phase&id=2579&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[Izumoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=phase&id=2579&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}} Tokurenba Kofun [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokurenba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokurenba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsue, Shimane]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Tokurenba Kofun]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:imported Kofun]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tokurenba Kofun]] [[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Heian period]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=phase&id=2579&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}} Tokurenba Kofun

Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=phase&id=2579&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}} Tokurenba Kofun

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is exposed in the center of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=phase&id=2579&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}} Tokurenba Kofun

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} is exposed in the center of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}.
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=phase&id=2579&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}} Tokurenba Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}

The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} is exposed in the center of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tokurenba Kofun]][[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tokurenba Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tokurenba Kofun | native_name =徳連場古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tokurenba Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Tokurenba Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tokurenba Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|0.7|N|133|00|50|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tokurenba Kofun'''|徳連場古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tamayu-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210980|title=徳連場古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tokurenba Kofun is located on the eastern hills of the Tamatsukuri hot spring town, just north of the Izumo Tamatsukuri Museum. It is a small circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 8.5 meters and a height of 1.5 meters, and is thought to have been constructed around the 5th century. Much of the mound has been eroded away, and the split bamboo-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} (or boat-shaped sarcophagus) made of white quartz andesitic {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} is exposed in the center of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The sarcophagus has a total length of 2.3 meters and a width of 0.8 meters, and the combination of lid and body is ''inroguchi'' style, with four hooks on the lid and two hooks on the body, which is rare in Shimane Prefecture. It is said that an iron sword and agate beads excavated from inside the sarcophagus, but this (and any other {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}) have been lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The area around this tumulus was a center for making ''[[magatama]]'', round and cylindrical beads from the end of the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.
The Imaichi Dainenji Kofun is located about one kilometer to the east of {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
